refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined productivity target by optimizing equipment operation, task scheduling, and resource allocation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level; decomposing it into sub-FRs focused on key productivity drivers (equipment operation efficiency, task scheduling, resource allocation) allows targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations; verify that each sub-FR contributes to meeting or exceeding the productivity target.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize equipment operation efficiency to maximize output per unit time.
FR2: Schedule tasks effectively to minimize idle time and maximize utilization.
FR3: Allocate resources dynamically to support continuous productivity.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement advanced vehicle control algorithms to enhance operation efficiency.
DP2: Develop a task scheduling system integrated with teleoperation capabilities.
DP3: Provide dynamic resource allocation through safety-aware coordination services.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement advanced vehicle control algorithms to enhance operation efficiency.

potential_sources: Interaction between vehicle control and teleoperation modules via Network Link and Tasking interfaces.
Coordination with Safety Services for safe operation.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically maps to vehicle operation efficiency and can be modularized with clear interfaces to teleoperation and safety.

assumptions_risks: Assumes vehicle control algorithms can be effectively integrated with teleoperation and safety modules.
Risk that poor interface definition may cause coupling and reduce modularity.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider refining DP2 and DP3 in subsequent decomposition levels to ensure clear separation and minimize coupling.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and software components responsible for vehicle control and operation efficiency.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle module sends operational data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task commands from Teleoperation and NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel for control and status updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of vehicle control DP; low diffusion risk.
Contains teleoperation logic; less suitable for vehicle control DP.
Focused on safety alerts; not suitable for vehicle control DP.
System-level module; may diffuse responsibility if used.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Vehicle module ensures focused ownership of vehicle control algorithms, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.
Risk of overlapping responsibilities if task scheduling logic is embedded in Vehicle.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify interface contracts and ensure task scheduling DP is assigned to Teleoperation or NGHE Solution to avoid diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (advanced vehicle control algorithms) logically satisfies the refined FR (optimize equipment operation efficiency) with clear mapping and verification approach.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion if task scheduling or resource allocation logic is mixed into Vehicle module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows high containment score for DP1, supporting clear ownership and modularity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring vehicle operational efficiency metrics and telemetry data.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of vehicle control DP to Vehicle module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Further decompose FR2 and FR3 with corresponding DP and IM assignments.
Define interface contracts explicitly to reduce diffusion risks.
Review task scheduling DP placement to avoid overlap with Vehicle module.

decision.justification: The current level decomposition and assignments are sound for the first sub-FR and DP, but further decomposition of remaining sub-FRs (task scheduling and resource allocation) is needed to complete the functional decomposition and ensure modular clarity.
next_level_sub_frs: FR2: Schedule tasks effectively to minimize idle time and maximize utilization.
FR3: Allocate resources dynamically to support continuous productivity.

revise.why: To decompose remaining productivity-related functional requirements for complete coverage and subsequent DP and IM assignments.

assumptions: Vehicle control algorithms can be modularized within Vehicle module.
Interfaces Telemetry, Tasking, and Network Link are sufficient for module communication.
open_risks: Potential diffusion if task scheduling and resource allocation are not clearly separated.
Safety criticality requires rigorous interface and module boundary definitions.
evidence_needed: Performance data demonstrating vehicle control improvements.
Interface specifications between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.
